description_long: This bollard is a robust, rectangular column constructed from large, block-like stones with a slightly irregular, hand-chiseled appearance. The main shaft is composed of dark blue-gray stone blocks, each with visible seams and subtle surface texturing, giving it a weathered, medieval look. The top of the bollard is capped with a thick, flat slab of lighter gray stone, featuring faint surface cracks and a beveled edge. The base is wider than the shaft, also made from the same light gray stone as the cap, and is set on a square platform that provides stability. The overall design is monolithic and solid, suggesting it is intended to serve as a sturdy barrier in urban environments. There are no visible openings, chains, or connectors, and the surface finish is matte with a slightly rough texture.
description: A solid, stone-block bollard with a dark blue-gray shaft, light gray flat cap, and a wide square base, featuring a weathered, hand-chiseled appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rectangular stone shaft with visible block seams and a prominent flat cap and base.
description_view_1: Side view is nearly identical to the front, highlighting the uniformity of the stone blocks and the cap's overhang.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with the same block pattern and cap, showing no additional features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again matching the other sides, emphasizing the symmetrical, monolithic design.
